title: A first estimation of gas hydrates offshore Patagonia (Chile)
abstract: The spatial distribution of gas hydrate in the accretionary prism and forearc sediments offshore western Patagonia (50°–57°S), landward of the Antarctica–South America plate boundary, is documented for the first time. BSR-derived heat flow values of 30–70 mW/m² are calculated. Average gas hydrate concentration is 3.4%–10% and average BSR thickness is ~290 m, with estimated methane of ~3.0 × 10¹³ m³.

location.description: Offshore western Patagonia (50°–57°S); first systematic gas hydrate study for southern Chilean Patagonian margin

seismic_lines.name: RC2902-790
seismic_lines.notes: R/V R.D. Conrad 1988; detailed velocity analysis and PreSDM; southern sector ~53°–57°S; strong continuous BSR

seismic_lines.name: IT95-251
seismic_lines.notes: R/V OGS-EXPLORA 1995; near FTJ, no BSR detected

seismic_lines.name: IT97-169
seismic_lines.notes: R/V OGS-EXPLORA 1997; deepest BSR 632 mbsf at ~55°S

seismic_lines.name: IT97-171
seismic_lines.notes: R/V OGS-EXPLORA 1997; strongest BSR, deep décollement near top of oceanic crust

seismic_lines.name: IT97-175
seismic_lines.notes: R/V OGS-EXPLORA 1997; southernmost profile ~57°S; shallowest BSR 286 mbsf

data.name: R/V R.D. Conrad RC2902 MCS Profile RC2902-790 — Southern Patagonia ~53°–57°S
data.description: RC2902-790 MCS profile (R/V R.D. Conrad 1988), 240 ch, 12.5 m group spacing, 15 s record length, offset up to 3300 m. Openly available at UTIG SDC (acknowledged). Used for detailed PreSDM velocity analysis and gas hydrate concentration estimation.

data.name: R/V OGS-EXPLORA IT95/IT97 MCS Profiles — Southern Patagonia 50°–57°S
data.description: Six profiles from Italian cruises IT95 (lines 247, 251) and IT97 (lines 168, 169, 171, 175) aboard R/V OGS-EXPLORA, 120 channels, 3000 m streamer, 36-airgun array, 50 m shot spacing. Used for BSR identification and heat flow mapping.

_data_note: RC2902 data openly available at http://www.ig.utexas.edu/ (UTIG SDC). OGS-EXPLORA data from OGS (Italian institution) — access unknown. Paper open access via OceanRep GEOMAR at https://oceanrep.geomar.de/id/eprint/43532/.

processing.summary: MCS reflection profiles from offshore western Patagonia (50°–57°S) were analyzed to identify and map the BSR distribution for the first time in this region. Velocity analysis provides gas hydrate concentration estimates (3.4%–10% total volume) and BSR-derived heat flow values (30–70 mW/m²). The large estimated methane volume (~3.0 × 10¹³ m³) makes this an important potential resource.
workflow: 1. Compilation of 7 MCS profiles from 3 cruises (RC2902, IT95, IT97)
2. BSR identification by reversed polarity relative to seafloor reflection
3. TWT to depth conversion using seawater velocity 1500 m/s
4. BSR depth and heat flow spatial mapping (colour-coded maps 50°–57°S)
5. Heat flow q calculation using Dickens & Quinby-Hunt (1994) BSR temperature-pressure function; k=0.85 W/mK (ODP Leg 202)
6. Iterative grid-based reflection tomography PreSDM (Woodward et al. 2008) on RC2902-790 for velocity model
7. Gas hydrate concentration estimation via modified Domenico approach (Tinivella & Carcione 2001; Tinivella 2002)
8. Average BSR depth and hydrate thickness calculation per CDP
9. Methane volume estimate using average concentration 3.4%, porosity 50%, total area 53,000 km²
processing.notes: Seven legacy seismic profiles from 3 cruises analyzed; PreSDM velocity analysis applied to RC2902-790 only

analysis_notes: PDF confirmed. Major corrections: vessel corrected from R/V SONNE to R/V R.D. Conrad RC2902 (1988) + R/V OGS-EXPLORA IT95 (1995) + IT97 (1997) — no R/V SONNE in this paper; year_acquired [1988,1995,1997]; seismic_lines updated to 7 named profiles (RC2902-790, IT95-247/-251, IT97-168/-169/-171/-175); channel_count=240 (RC2902)/120 (OGS); record_length_s=15 (RC2902); data entries split — RC2902 open at UTIG SDC http://www.ig.utexas.edu/, OGS-EXPLORA unknown; software=GMT; migration_type corrected from PSSDM to PreSDM; workflow expanded to 9 steps.
